game_name: Solar Jetman: Hunt for the Golden Warpship

design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate gravity-affected caverns on multiple planets, collect Golden Warpship pieces and fuel, manage inertia physics and tow-cable drag mechanics, return items to mothership
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Gravity and inertia management vs. fuel scarcity and enemy threats; towing items increases vulnerability through drag
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Jetman piloting a nimble space pod through alien caverns, scavenging for legendary starship components while mastering physics-based flight
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game prioritizes physics-based challenge (variable gravity per planet, inertia without drag) over pattern memorization; refuses to simplify controls despite difficulty. Deliberately punishes item collection with drag mechanics. Committed to gravity as core design pillar rather than afterthought

difficulty.lives_system: Player loses life when dying outside pod; can eject from destroyed pod and walk in vulnerable space suit to attempt pod recovery
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-planet structure allows progression through multiple worlds, but gravity changes every level making stable flight increasingly difficult
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player can spend earned points to purchase power-ups for pod between stages; pod controls are intentionally physics-heavy (inertia, varying gravity pulls) giving skilled players advantage through mastery

progression.upgrade_system: Points earned from collecting valuables and destroying enemies can be spent after every other stage to purchase power-ups for Jetman's pod
progression.unlock_mechanism: Each planet contains one piece of Golden Warpship that must be collected and returned to mothership to progress to next planet; 12 main planets plus 1 hidden planet (Dryn Kup Leez, apparently planned but unfinished)
replayability_hooks: Password system allowing mid-game starts at various planets
Variable gravity on each planet creates different flight challenges on replay
Optional bonus stage accessible via wormhole on Planet 1 (Preludon)
Point accumulation and power-up purchasing rewards skilled players

world_building_techniques: Series continuity: sequel to Lunar Jetman with established protagonist Jetman
MacGuffin quest structure: hunt for Golden Warpship pieces across alien worlds
Planet-based world design: 13 distinct cavern systems with unique gravity properties suggest alien diversity
Item lore: wormholes and tow-cable mechanics imply functional sci-fi universe logic

danger_signals: Enemy-infested caverns on all planets create persistent hostile environments
Gravity variations serve as environmental hazard rather than faction signature
Wormholes as transport mechanism could indicate alien infrastructure or natural phenomena
environmental_storytelling: Each planet's unique gravity tells implicit story about world properties
Cavern layouts with item placements and enemy positions suggest exploration reward structure
Mothership as safe zone creates risk/reward dynamic for venturing deeper
Golden Warpship fragments scattered across multiple worlds implies ancient/legendary artifact
